blocks:
Ayahs

إِذَا جَآءَ نَصْرُ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلْفَتْحُ (۱)
When Allah’s help comes with victory, (1)
وَرَأَيْتَ ٱلنَّاسَ يَدْخُلُونَ فِى دِينِ ٱللَّهِ أَفْوَاجًا (۲)
and you see the people entering Allah’s religion in throngs, (2)
فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَٱسْتَغْفِرْهُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ تَوَّابًۢا (۳)
celebrate the praise of your Lord, and plead to Him for forgiveness. Indeed, He is all-clement. (3)

Key Points

Most commentators consider this Surah related to the time of the Conquest of Mecca (Fath Makkah) in the eighth year of the Hijra, when a group of people embraced Islam due to its truthfulness, and others feigned Islam out of fear, such as Abu Sufyan, about whom Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «ما اسلموا ولكن استسلموا و اسرّوا الكفر»(227) They became Muslim outwardly but concealed disbelief in their hearts.

The Quran mentions two types of entry (dokhul) for faith: one is the entry of people into the religion, as stated in this Surah: «یدخلون فى دین اللّه», and the other is the entry of faith into the hearts of people, as stated in Surah Al-Hujurat: «و لمّا یدخل الایمان فى قلوبكم»(228) Faith has not yet entered your hearts.

We read in a tradition (hadith): When Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام appears, the people of the world will embrace faith in him in throngs. (229)

The Quran has emphasized the glorification (Tasbih) of Allah more than the magnification (Takbir) and praise (Tahmid) of Him. The word Tasbih appears in the Quran more often than the words Takbir and Tahmid.

We read in a tradition: During the Conquest of Mecca, the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله first entered the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) and performed prayer. (230)

The help (Nasr) of Allah is contingent upon people supporting His religion. «ان تنصروا اللّه ینصركم» (231)

The word Tawwab (Oft-Returning/Acceptor of Repentance) appears eleven times in the Quran: nine times accompanied by the word Rahmah (mercy) «توّاباً رحیماً», once accompanied by Hikmah (wisdom) (232), and once absolutely (without accompaniment) in this Surah.

After the Conquest of Mecca, the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله declared a general amnesty and forgave everyone, and Mecca was conquered without bloodshed. Even when the Prophet saw Sa'd ibn Ubadah raising the slogan of revenge, saying «الیوم یوم الملحمة» (meaning: today we take revenge), the Prophet took the banner from him and gave it to Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, instructing him to change the slogan and say: «الیوم یوم المرحمة» (today is the day of mercy, not revenge).

True conquest and victory come from Allah; therefore, do not rely on tools, equipment, or human power, for you might have all the resources and still be defeated. «و ما النّصر الاّ من عند اللّه» (233)

In the Conquest of Mecca, the hopes of the idolaters turned into despair, and the center of polytheism was transformed into a base of monotheism. Obstacles that stood in the way of the believers were removed, Islam was stabilized, and it became ready for export to other countries.

The command for seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) is either for greater illumination of the heart, or for removing the arrogance of victory, or for the sins of the Ummah, or due to the Prophet's high level of knowledge concerning Allah.

A brief look at the Conquest of Mecca

After the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, since the polytheists broke the covenant, the Prophet gathered a force and conquered Mecca without bloodshed. The details of the event are as follows:

The Muslims camped a few kilometers outside Mecca. Abu Sufyan, who had left Mecca secretly at night to scout, met with Abbas, the Prophet's uncle. Abbas said: The Prophet has come to you with ten thousand soldiers. Abu Sufyan was terrified and sought refuge with Abbas, and the Prophet accepted the protection granted by Abbas.

The Prophet announced: Whoever takes refuge in the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram), or goes to the house of Abu Sufyan, or stays in his own house, is safe.

The Prophet told Abbas: Take Abu Sufyan to the narrow pass that is the gateway to Mecca so that he may see the Divine armies passing through. Abu Sufyan was terrified upon seeing this maneuver and told Abbas: The sovereignty of your nephew has become very great. Abbas replied: It is not sovereignty, but prophethood.

Then Abbas told him: Go to the people of Mecca and prevent them from confronting the army of Islam. Abu Sufyan entered the Sacred Mosque and told the people: Muhammad is entering Mecca with a multitude that you cannot confront, so take refuge in the Sacred Mosque, or my house, or your own houses, and embrace Islam so that you may remain safe.

In any case, the Holy Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله entered Mecca, recalling the day He had secretly left Mecca, and performed prostration (Sajdah) in gratitude for this victory. Then the Prophet entered the Sacred Mosque reciting Surah Al-Fath, and the Muslims accompanying Him chanted Takbir (Allahu Akbar).

The Prophet approached the Kaaba and began smashing the idols, saying: «جاء الحق و زهق الباطل انّ الباطل كان زهوقا»(234) (The truth has come and falsehood has vanished; indeed, falsehood is bound to vanish.) Since the Prophet’s hand could not reach the large idols on top of the Kaaba, He commanded Hadrat Ali عليه السلام to place his foot on the Prophet’s shoulder and topple the idols.

He then took hold of the ring of the Kaaba and asked the people of Mecca: What do you expect from me today? They replied: Forgiveness. Tears gathered in the Prophet’s eyes, and the people wept. The Prophet spoke the words of Joseph, who told his brothers: «لاتثریب علیكم الیوم»(235) (No reproach shall be upon you this day), and said: You are all free.

He then recited the phrases: «لااله الاّ اللّه وحده وحده انجز وعده نصر عبده...» (There is no god but Allah, the One, the One; He fulfilled His promise, He helped His servant...) and declared: Let no one speak of past bloodshed or looted property. All past files are closed, and everyone is included in the general amnesty. Now enter a new life.

227) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 32, p. 325. 228) Al-Hujurat, 14. 229) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 230) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqaiq. 231) Muhammad, 7. 232) An-Nisa, 17. 233) Al Imran, 126. 234) Al-Isra, 81. 235) Yusuf, 92.

Messages

1- Victory is achieved under the shade of Divine help. «اذا جاء نصر اللّه و الفتح»

2- The destruction of the leaders of disbelief and polytheism paves the way for people to enter monotheism and the worship of the One God. «اذا جاء نصر اللّه... النّاس یدخلون فى دین اللّه»

3- Muslims have a duty to strive to the best of their ability and capacity to prepare the ground for others to enter Islam. «اذا جاء نصر اللّه... النّاس یدخلون فى دین اللّه»

4- If obstacles are removed, people will embrace faith in throngs. «یدخلون فى دین اللّه افواجا»

5- Before the Conquest of Mecca, people became Muslim one by one, but after the Conquest of Mecca, they came in throngs. «یدخلون فى دین اللّه افواجاً»

6- Do not let conquest and victory make you arrogant, for everything is from Allah. «اذا جاء نصر اللّه... فسبّح بحمد ربّك»

7- The purification (Tanzih) of Allah from fault, defect, and oppression must be accompanied by gratitude for His blessings and praise of Him. «فسبّح بحمد ربّك»

8- Gratitude, praise, and glorification of Allah are prerequisites for seeking forgiveness (Istighfar). «فسبح بحمد واستغفره»

9- When the righteous attain power, instead of arrogance and heedlessness, they perfume their souls and spirits with the remembrance of Allah. «اذا جاء نصر اللّه... فسبّح بحمد ربّك»

10- Allah is pure (Munazzah) from abandoning the supporters of His religion. «اذا جاء نصر اللّه... فسبّح»

11- Everyone, even the Prophet, no matter how much he glorifies and praises, should conclude it by seeking forgiveness. «واستغفره»

12- Accepting repentance is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah. «انّه كان توّاباً»
